items.question: Which hyperparameter sets the size of the high-dimensional k-NN neighborhood graph in UMAP?
items.explanation: n_neighbors controls how many neighbors each point links to when building the fuzzy graph; min_dist shapes low-D packing, n_components is output dimensionality, perplexity belongs to t-SNE.

items.question: You want tighter, more separated clusters in a 2D UMAP plot without changing the high-D graph. What should you try first?
items.explanation: min_dist controls embedding-space tightness; n_neighbors changes the graph itself, and removing scaling changes neighbor links.

items.question: Compared to PCA, UMAP is primarily suited for:
options: Non-linear manifold visualization when linear projections smear structure
Maximizing explained variance with interpretable loadings
Guaranteeing exact preservation of all pairwise high-D distances
Supervised label prediction without a separate classifier
items.answer: Non-linear manifold visualization when linear projections smear structure
items.explanation: PCA is the linear variance baseline; UMAP targets neighborhood structure on curved manifolds—see PCA topic for variance interpretation.

items.question: Small n_neighbors (e.g., 5) tends to produce embeddings that:
options: Emphasize fine local structure, sometimes at the cost of global continuity
Ignore all local neighborhoods and only preserve global distances exactly
Remove the need for any distance metric
items.answer: Emphasize fine local structure, sometimes at the cost of global continuity
items.explanation: Small neighborhoods make the fuzzy graph local-heavy; large n_neighbors pulls in distant points for more global connectivity.
